T0253.title: Comment-Thread Lead Diversion
T0253.definition: A covert traffic diversion tactic where two accounts stage a question-and-answer exchange in a comment section to subtly guide users toward a product or service.
T0253.description: In this operation, one account poses as an ordinary user asking a question, while another account replies as a satisfied customer, recommending the target product or service. This creates a false impression of genuine word-of-mouth endorsement. The staged dialogue effectively bypasses platform scrutiny of direct advertising and lowers user skepticism. It is commonly used for diverting traffic to 'black five' categories like medical aesthetics, health supplements, and financial scams.
T0253.usageExample: "The A-B diversion for that acne treatment is working well lately. The sock puppet asks 'How to get rid of acne scars,' and the main account replies 'Use XX Gel.' The conversion rate is very high."
